en_definition: to make a particular action seem reasonable or necessary
example1: The results warrant further investigation before the paper can be published.
example1_ru: Результаты служат основанием для дальнейшего исследования перед публикацией статьи.
example2: Such a serious error warrants an immediate correction.

en_definition: to create or express an idea, plan, or rule in a clear way
example1: The team formulated a new hypothesis after the first trial.
example1_ru: После первого испытания команда сформулировала новую гипотезу.
example2: You need to formulate your argument more precisely.

en_definition: to show or point out that something is likely to be true
example1: The results indicate a clear improvement over the previous method.
example1_ru: Результаты указывают на явное улучшение по сравнению с предыдущим методом.
example2: A rising temperature can indicate a fault in the system.

en_definition: a typical example or pattern of how something is thought about
example1: The discovery led to a new paradigm in physics.
example1_ru: Открытие привело к новой парадигме в физике.
example2: This model represents a shift away from the old paradigm.

en_definition: a standard used to judge or decide something
example1: Accuracy is the main criterion for grading this assignment.
example1_ru: Точность — главный критерий оценки этого задания.
example2: Each criterion is weighted differently in the final score.

en_definition: to be a sign of something or to mean something
example1: A red flag on the graph signifies an error in the data.
example1_ru: Красный флажок на графике означает ошибку в данных.
example2: This change signifies a major shift in university policy.

en_definition: to use something or someone in place of another
example1: Substitute the known values into the equation and solve.
example1_ru: Подставьте известные значения в уравнение и решите его.
example2: You can substitute margarine for butter in this recipe.

en_definition: used to introduce a fact that is accepted as true for an argument
example1: Given that the sample is small, the results are not conclusive.
example1_ru: Учитывая, что выборка небольшая, результаты не окончательны.
example2: Given that costs are rising, the budget must be revised.

en_definition: concerning or in relation to a particular thing
example1: With respect to the deadline, no extensions will be granted.
example1_ru: Что касается срока сдачи, продления предоставлены не будут.
example2: The two groups differ with respect to average income.

en_definition: to decide something after considering all the evidence
example1: Researchers conclude that the treatment is generally safe.
example1_ru: Исследователи делают вывод, что лечение в целом безопасно.
example2: The essay concludes with a summary of the main points.

en_definition: to state something firmly and confidently as true
example1: The author asserts that early intervention improves outcomes.
example1_ru: Автор утверждает, что раннее вмешательство улучшает результаты.
example2: She asserted her right to see the full report.

en_definition: to say that something is caused by a particular thing
example1: Scientists attribute the rise in temperature to human activity.
example1_ru: Учёные приписывают повышение температуры деятельности человека.
example2: She attributes her success to hard work and good mentors.

en_definition: to state firmly and publicly that something is true
example1: The committee affirmed that the results met the required standard.
example1_ru: Комиссия подтвердила, что результаты соответствуют требуемому стандарту.
example2: She affirmed her commitment to finishing the research project.

en_definition: an argument that opposes another argument
example1: A strong essay always addresses the main counterargument.
example1_ru: В сильном эссе всегда рассматривается главный контраргумент.
example2: He raised a counterargument that the professor had not considered.

en_definition: an idea or opinion that contrasts with another one
example1: Her essay offers a useful counterpoint to the main theory.
example1_ru: Её эссе предлагает полезную альтернативную точку зрения на основную теорию.
example2: The debate needs a clear counterpoint to stay balanced.

en_definition: a statement or idea that is put forward for consideration
example1: The professor asked us to evaluate the central proposition.
example1_ru: Профессор попросил нас оценить центральный тезис.
example2: This proposition needs more evidence before it can be accepted.

en_definition: a publicly stated opinion on a particular issue
example1: The report outlines the committee's stance on climate policy.
example1_ru: В отчёте изложена позиция комитета по климатической политике.
example2: Her stance on the issue changed after reading new studies.

en_definition: a particular way of thinking about a subject
example1: Try to present both viewpoints fairly in your essay.
example1_ru: Постарайтесь честно представить обе точки зрения в своём эссе.
example2: From an economic viewpoint, the plan makes sense.

en_definition: a particular way of considering an issue
example1: From a scientific standpoint, the claim is unproven.
example1_ru: С научной точки зрения это утверждение не доказано.
example2: He explained the problem from a practical standpoint.

en_definition: a general statement based on a limited number of facts or cases
example1: It is risky to make a generalization from such a small sample.
example1_ru: Делать обобщение на основе такой малой выборки рискованно.
example2: The article avoids broad generalizations about the whole population.

en_definition: to form a general rule or conclusion from specific examples
example1: You cannot generalize about a whole city from one street.
example1_ru: Нельзя делать обобщение о целом городе на основе одной улицы.
example2: The study generalizes its findings to all age groups.

en_definition: to recognize the difference between two things
example1: It is important to distinguish between correlation and causation.
example1_ru: Важно различать корреляцию и причинно-следственную связь.
example2: Can you distinguish between these two chemical compounds?

en_definition: to recognize or show the difference between two or more things
example1: This test helps doctors differentiate between similar illnesses.
example1_ru: Этот тест помогает врачам различать похожие заболевания.
example2: Students must differentiate between fact and opinion in the text.

en_definition: to be greater or more important than something else
example1: The benefits of the new policy outweigh its costs.
example1_ru: Преимущества новой политики перевешивают её издержки.
example2: In this case, the risks clearly outweigh the rewards.

en_definition: to balance the effect of something by having an opposite effect
example1: Higher sales offset the rise in production costs.
example1_ru: Более высокие продажи компенсировали рост производственных издержек.
example2: The scholarship offsets part of the tuition fee.

en_definition: to happen at the same time as something else, or to match it
example1: The exam period coincides with the busiest week at work.
example1_ru: Период экзаменов совпадает с самой напряжённой неделей на работе.
example2: Her findings coincide with those of earlier studies.

en_definition: to behave or be produced according to accepted rules or standards
example1: The report must conform to the department's formatting guidelines.
example1_ru: Отчёт должен соответствовать требованиям к оформлению кафедры.
example2: The new device conforms to international safety standards.

en_definition: to arrange data in rows and columns in a table
example1: Tabulate the results before writing your discussion section.
example1_ru: Сведите результаты в таблицу перед тем, как писать раздел обсуждения.
example2: The survey answers were tabulated by age group.

en_definition: to arrange items in order according to a particular quality
example1: Rank the factors from most to least important.
example1_ru: Расположите факторы по значимости — от самого важного к наименее важному.
example2: The university is ranked among the top ten in the country.

en_definition: the sentence that states the main idea of a paragraph
example1: Every paragraph should begin with a clear topic sentence.
example1_ru: Каждый абзац должен начинаться с чёткого тематического предложения.
example2: The topic sentence tells the reader what the paragraph is about.

en_definition: facts or examples that support a claim or argument
example1: The claim needs more supporting evidence to be convincing.
example1_ru: Это утверждение нуждается в дополнительных подтверждающих доказательствах, чтобы быть убедительным.
example2: Each paragraph should include supporting evidence from the text.

en_definition: an argument that proves another argument is wrong
example1: The final section of the essay presents a rebuttal of the opposing view.
example1_ru: В заключительной части эссе приводится опровержение противоположной точки зрения.
example2: Her rebuttal was based on more recent research data.

en_definition: the process of repeating a study to check if the results are the same
example1: Replication of the experiment confirmed the original findings.
example1_ru: Повторение эксперимента подтвердило первоначальные результаты.
example2: Journals increasingly require replication before accepting new claims.

en_definition: the factor in an experiment that is measured and expected to change
example1: The dependent variable in this study is reaction time.
example1_ru: Зависимая переменная в этом исследовании — время реакции.
example2: Plot the dependent variable on the vertical axis of the graph.

en_definition: a group in an experiment that does not receive the treatment being tested
example1: The control group received a placebo instead of the drug.
example1_ru: Контрольная группа получала плацебо вместо лекарства.
example2: Comparing results with the control group shows the treatment's effect.

en_definition: the number of people or items included in a study
example1: A larger sample size makes the results more reliable.
example1_ru: Больший размер выборки делает результаты более надёжными.
example2: The study's sample size was too small to draw firm conclusions.

en_definition: a person who answers a survey or questionnaire
example1: Most respondents said they preferred online lectures to in-person classes.
example1_ru: Большинство респондентов сказали, что предпочитают онлайн-лекции очным занятиям.
example2: The survey included two hundred respondents from different faculties.

en_definition: a group of people with a shared characteristic studied over time
example1: The study followed a cohort of students for four years.
example1_ru: Исследование наблюдало за когортой студентов в течение четырёх лет.
example2: Researchers compared two cohorts born a decade apart.

en_definition: the range of possible error in a survey's results
example1: The poll has a margin of error of three percent.
example1_ru: У опроса погрешность составляет три процента.
example2: Always report the margin of error alongside the results.

en_definition: a range of values likely to contain the true result of a study
example1: The confidence interval shows how precise the estimate is.
example1_ru: Доверительный интервал показывает, насколько точна оценка.
example2: A narrower confidence interval usually means a larger sample.

en_definition: unlikely to have happened by chance according to statistical tests
example1: The difference between the two groups was statistically significant.
example1_ru: Разница между двумя группами была статистически значимой.
example2: The results were not statistically significant enough to publish.

en_definition: a number that shows how strongly two variables are related
example1: A correlation coefficient close to one shows a strong link.
example1_ru: Коэффициент корреляции, близкий к единице, показывает сильную связь.
example2: We calculated the correlation coefficient between height and weight.

en_definition: to make data unbalanced or distorted in a particular direction
example1: One extreme value can skew the average of a small sample.
example1_ru: Одно экстремальное значение может исказить среднее значение небольшой выборки.
example2: The survey results were skewed by an unrepresentative sample.

en_definition: a measure of how spread out the values in a dataset are
example1: A small standard deviation means the data points are close together.
example1_ru: Небольшое стандартное отклонение означает, что данные близки друг к другу.
example2: Calculate the standard deviation of the sample before comparing groups.

en_definition: to estimate unknown values by extending known data or trends
example1: We extrapolated from the first week's sales to predict monthly totals.
example1_ru: Мы экстраполировали данные за первую неделю продаж, чтобы спрогнозировать месячные итоги.
example2: It is risky to extrapolate from such a small dataset.

en_definition: a number placed before a variable to show a fixed relationship
example1: The coefficient in front of x determines the slope of the line.
example1_ru: Коэффициент перед x определяет наклон прямой.
example2: Increase the coefficient to make the reaction proceed faster.

en_definition: the point where a line crosses an axis on a graph
example1: The y-intercept shows the value when x equals zero.
example1_ru: Точка пересечения с осью y показывает значение при x, равном нулю.
example2: Find the intercept before drawing the rest of the graph.

en_definition: a line that a curve approaches but never touches
example1: The graph approaches the asymptote but never crosses it.
example1_ru: График приближается к асимптоте, но никогда не пересекает её.
example2: The horizontal asymptote shows the long-term value of the function.

en_definition: changing in a fixed ratio with another quantity
example1: The force is proportional to the acceleration of the object.
example1_ru: Сила пропорциональна ускорению объекта.
example2: Pay is proportional to the number of hours worked.

en_definition: changing so that as one quantity increases, the other decreases at a fixed rate
example1: Pressure is inversely proportional to volume at constant temperature.
example1_ru: Давление обратно пропорционально объёму при постоянной температуре.
example2: Speed is inversely proportional to travel time over a fixed distance.

en_definition: a standard used to compare or judge other things
example1: The new test results set a benchmark for future studies.
example1_ru: Результаты нового теста задают ориентир для будущих исследований.
example2: This score is used as a benchmark for admission.

en_definition: a starting point used for later comparisons
example1: Researchers measured a baseline before starting the treatment.
example1_ru: Исследователи измерили исходный уровень перед началом лечения.
example2: Compare the new data with the baseline from last year.

en_definition: the highest possible value that a quantity can take
example1: The upper bound of the estimate is set by the largest sample value.
example1_ru: Верхняя граница оценки задаётся наибольшим значением в выборке.
example2: Set an upper bound to avoid unrealistic predictions.

en_definition: a visible sign that a quality, idea, or condition exists
example1: The rash is a manifestation of an allergic reaction.
example1_ru: Сыпь — это проявление аллергической реакции.
example2: Rising sea levels are one manifestation of climate change.

en_definition: a fact or condition connected with an event or situation
example1: Under normal circumstances, the reaction would take longer.
example1_ru: При обычных обстоятельствах реакция заняла бы больше времени.
example2: Explain the circumstances that led to the delay.

en_definition: happening at the same time as something else
example1: Students may take two concurrent courses this semester.
example1_ru: В этом семестре студенты могут посещать два параллельных курса.
example2: The experiment ran concurrent trials to save time.

en_definition: following one after another without interruption
example1: She submitted assignments on time for six consecutive weeks.
example1_ru: Она сдавала задания вовремя шесть недель подряд.
example2: The lab requires three consecutive days of measurements.

en_definition: arranged for the present, possibly to be changed later
example1: The university sent a provisional offer pending final grades.
example1_ru: Университет прислал предварительное предложение о зачислении до получения итоговых оценок.
example2: These are provisional figures and may be revised next month.

en_definition: not fixed or certain; likely to change
example1: We have a tentative schedule for the group project.
example1_ru: У нас есть предварительный график для группового проекта.
example2: The findings are tentative until more data is collected.

en_definition: proving something clearly and leaving no doubt
example1: The test provided conclusive proof of the theory.
example1_ru: Тест дал убедительное доказательство теории.
example2: The evidence is not yet conclusive enough to publish.

en_definition: not leading to a definite result or decision
example1: The study's results were inconclusive and require further research.
example1_ru: Результаты исследования оказались неубедительными и требуют дальнейшего изучения.
example2: An inconclusive test means we need to repeat the experiment.

en_definition: having only one clear meaning or interpretation
example1: The rules must be unambiguous so that everyone understands them equally.
example1_ru: Правила должны быть недвусмысленными, чтобы все понимали их одинаково.
example2: Give an unambiguous answer to the examiner's question.

en_definition: being the true, basic cause or nature of something, even if hidden
example1: The underlying cause of the problem was a software bug.
example1_ru: Основной причиной проблемы была ошибка в программном обеспечении.
example2: Try to identify the underlying assumption in the argument.

en_definition: existing in a person or animal from birth rather than being learned
example1: Some researchers believe language ability is partly innate.
example1_ru: Некоторые исследователи считают, что способность к языку отчасти врождённая.
example2: The innate curiosity of children drives early learning.

en_definition: belonging naturally to something as an essential feature
example1: The intrinsic value of the material makes it expensive.
example1_ru: Внутренняя ценность материала делает его дорогим.
example2: Curiosity is an intrinsic part of scientific research.

en_definition: forming a necessary base or core part of something
example1: Understanding algebra is fundamental to studying physics at university level.
example1_ru: Понимание алгебры фундаментально важно для изучения физики на университетском уровне.
example2: The course covers the fundamental principles of chemistry.

en_definition: so small that it is not worth considering
example1: The difference between the two results was negligible.
example1_ru: Разница между двумя результатами была незначительной.
example2: Air resistance is negligible in this calculation.

en_definition: small in amount or importance
example1: There was only a marginal improvement in test scores.
example1_ru: Улучшение результатов теста было лишь незначительным.
example2: The change had a marginal effect on the overall outcome.

en_definition: large enough to be important or noticeable
example1: The new method saved a considerable amount of time.
example1_ru: Новый метод сэкономил значительное количество времени.
example2: There is considerable evidence to support the theory.

en_definition: existing very commonly in a particular place or time
example1: This disease is more prevalent in colder climates.
example1_ru: Это заболевание более распространено в холодном климате.
example2: Online learning has become prevalent since the pandemic.

en_definition: most common or most widely accepted at a particular time
example1: The prevailing view among scientists supports this theory.
example1_ru: Преобладающая точка зрения среди учёных подтверждает эту теорию.
example2: The prevailing wind direction affects local weather patterns.

en_definition: existing or happening over a large area or among many people
example1: There is widespread support for the new curriculum.
example1_ru: Новая учебная программа пользуется широкой поддержкой.
example2: The technology has become widespread in recent years.

en_definition: never having happened or existed before
example1: The university saw an unprecedented number of applications this year.
example1_ru: В этом году университет получил беспрецедентное количество заявок.
example2: Such rapid growth in the field is unprecedented.

en_definition: having a quality that makes something easily recognizable
example1: This mineral has a distinctive blue color that is easy to recognize.
example1_ru: Этот минерал имеет отличительный синий цвет, который легко узнать.
example2: Each species has a distinctive pattern of behavior.

en_definition: based on what is generally done or believed
example1: The lab uses a conventional method to measure density.
example1_ru: Лаборатория использует традиционный метод измерения плотности.
example2: This approach is more effective than conventional teaching.

en_definition: different from what is usually done or expected
example1: The professor used an unconventional approach to teach statistics.
example1_ru: Профессор использовал нестандартный подход к преподаванию статистики.
example2: Her unconventional solution surprised the whole team.

en_definition: considering or including every possible detail or option
example1: The librarian did an exhaustive search for relevant journal articles.
example1_ru: Библиотекарь провёл исчерпывающий поиск соответствующих журнальных статей.
example2: This is not an exhaustive list of all possible errors.

en_definition: covering a large area, range, or amount
example1: The research required extensive fieldwork over two years.
example1_ru: Исследование потребовало обширной полевой работы в течение двух лет.
example2: She has extensive experience in laboratory research.

en_definition: limited in extent, size, or scope
example1: Access to the lab is restricted to registered students.
example1_ru: Доступ в лабораторию ограничен только зарегистрированными студентами.
example2: The experiment used a restricted range of temperatures.

en_definition: a brief summary of the main points of a text or plan
example1: Write a short synopsis of the article for the seminar.
example1_ru: Напишите краткое содержание статьи для семинара.
example2: The synopsis gave a clear idea of the whole report.

en_definition: able to be measured or expressed as a number
example1: The study focuses on quantifiable outcomes such as test scores.
example1_ru: Исследование сосредоточено на измеримых результатах, таких как баллы тестов.
example2: Not every benefit of the program is easily quantifiable.

en_definition: not favoring one side more than another; fair
example1: An impartial reviewer assessed each application on its merits.
example1_ru: Беспристрастный рецензент оценивал каждую заявку по её достоинствам.
example2: The judge is expected to remain impartial throughout the case.

en_definition: based on clear thinking and reason rather than emotion
example1: A rational decision should be based on the available data.
example1_ru: Рациональное решение должно основываться на имеющихся данных.
example2: It is more rational to save money than spend it all.

en_definition: well organized so that the parts fit together as a whole
example1: Use linking words to make your paragraph more cohesive.
example1_ru: Используйте связующие слова, чтобы абзац был более целостным.
example2: The team worked as a cohesive unit throughout the project.

en_definition: always behaving or happening in the same way
example1: The results were consistent across all three trials.
example1_ru: Результаты были последовательными во всех трёх испытаниях.
example2: Her argument is consistent with the evidence provided.

en_definition: seeming reasonable or probable
example1: The professor asked for a plausible explanation of the result.
example1_ru: Профессор попросил дать правдоподобное объяснение результата.
example2: Several plausible theories could explain this phenomenon.

en_definition: able to be reached or accomplished
example1: Set attainable goals for the first semester so you stay motivated.
example1_ru: Ставьте достижимые цели на первый семестр, чтобы сохранять мотивацию.
example2: The target was ambitious but ultimately attainable.

en_definition: able to be believed or trusted
example1: Use credible sources when writing an academic paper.
example1_ru: При написании научной работы используйте заслуживающие доверия источники.
example2: Her explanation seemed credible given the circumstances.

en_definition: based on good judgment or valid reasoning
example1: The argument is logically sound and well supported.
example1_ru: Аргумент логически обоснован и хорошо подкреплён.
example2: They made a sound decision based on the data available.

en_definition: having a mistake or weakness that reduces value or quality
example1: The original design was flawed and had to be revised.
example1_ru: Первоначальный проект был с изъяном, и его пришлось пересмотреть.
example2: Critics pointed out that the study's methodology was flawed.

en_definition: able to recover quickly from difficult conditions or damage
example1: The material is resilient enough to withstand repeated stress.
example1_ru: Материал достаточно устойчив, чтобы выдерживать многократные нагрузки.
example2: A resilient economy recovers faster after a crisis.

en_definition: enough or more than enough for a particular purpose
example1: There was ample time to finish the exam before the deadline.
example1_ru: Времени, чтобы закончить экзамен до дедлайна, было более чем достаточно.
example2: The library provides ample resources for independent research.

en_definition: not enough for a particular purpose
example1: There is insufficient evidence to support this claim.
example1_ru: Доказательств недостаточно, чтобы подтвердить это утверждение.
example2: The funding was insufficient to complete the whole project.

en_definition: more than what is necessary, normal, or reasonable
example1: The report criticized the excessive use of plastic packaging.
example1_ru: В отчёте критиковалось чрезмерное использование пластиковой упаковки.
example2: Excessive heat can damage the sensitive equipment.

en_definition: not needed because it repeats something already said or used
example1: Remove any redundant words to make the essay more concise.
example1_ru: Удалите все лишние слова, чтобы эссе стало более лаконичным.
example2: The second sensor is redundant since the first one works fine.

en_definition: the best or most effective possible under the circumstances
example1: Researchers looked for the optimal temperature for the reaction.
example1_ru: Исследователи искали оптимальную температуру для реакции.
example2: This method gives the optimal balance between speed and accuracy.

en_definition: achieving results with little wasted time or effort
example1: This is a more efficient way to organize your notes.
example1_ru: Это более эффективный способ организовать свои конспекты.
example2: The new engine is far more fuel efficient.

en_definition: producing good results or an improvement
example1: Regular exercise is beneficial for both body and mind.
example1_ru: Регулярные физические упражнения полезны как для тела, так и для ума.
example2: Working in groups can be beneficial for learning new skills.

en_definition: causing harm or damage
example1: Lack of sleep can be detrimental to academic performance.
example1_ru: Недостаток сна может быть пагубным для успеваемости.
example2: The chemical spill had a detrimental effect on the local ecosystem.

en_definition: having the opposite effect from the one intended
example1: Skipping sleep before an exam is often counterproductive.
example1_ru: Отказ от сна перед экзаменом часто бывает контрпродуктивным.
example2: Excessive rules can be counterproductive in a creative team.
